name: RADARSAT Constellation Mission

overview.value: The RADARSAT Constellation Mission (RCM) is Canada's third generation of Earth observation satellites, launched June 12, 2019 with three identical satellites working together. RCM is the first mission in the RADARSAT program to carry dual payloads, combining a SAR instrument with an Automatic Identification System (AIS) instrument for ship detection. The three satellites cover Canada and areas of interest elsewhere more frequently than earlier RADARSAT missions.

revisit.value: daily global re-look in Medium Resolution 50 m mode; four-day exact repeat cycle for InSAR coherent change detection

base.reason: eoPortal mission page (ESA-run aggregator); page states 'average daily global re-look capability in medium 50 m resolution mode' and 'RCM provides a four-day exact revisit, allowing coherent change detection using an InSAR mode' (registry v2 EO spec backfill, cite-facts-only per terms)
eoPortal mission page (ESA-run aggregator); page states 'Radar center frequency: 5.405 GHz (C-band)' (registry v2 EO spec backfill)

imaging_modes.mode: Spotlight (1 m azimuth x 3 m range)

notes: Canadian SAR constellation operated for CSA; MDA is prime and commercial distributor. Seeded for the Orbits surface on Florian's instruction (2026-07-05); v2 EO capability fields filled 2026-07-09 from eoPortal (page last updated 2026-03-09). Only two modes carried a clean per-mode resolution/swath pairing on the page; the full mode table (Table 6) was not legibly extractable, and the source's overall range figures (5-50 m resolution, 20-350 km swath, 500 km accessible swath) are ranges per CLAUDE.md's no-range-reduction rule, so constellation-level resolution_m/swath_km stay null.
